title: Flinders and the Cape: A One-Night Reset
dek: For people who want salt air, one excellent meal, and just enough structure to stop planning.

stops.note: Check in early enough to make the village feel like your own base rather than a stopover.
Take the easy dramatic option first. The view lands quickly and resets the nervous system.
Book ahead and let dinner be the event.
Do the longer coastal walk in the morning while your legs and weather are both fresh.
Finish with a late lunch and art before heading home.

editorNote: The one-night Peninsula trip only works if you resist the temptation to cram it full. This version stays south, keeps the driving short, and gives equal weight to coastline and lunch. Flinders gives you a clean village base. Cape Schanck supplies the weather and the scale. Tedesca handles dinner. By the time you are leaving Pt. Leo after lunch the next day, it feels like you were away for longer than twenty-four hours.
